response:
sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. she decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. if a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? remember to apply the discount to each item separately. the pen is mightier than sword

*** 

the promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items. the handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be discounted by 15% to $170. the factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will be discounted by 15% to $127.50. the total amount the customer will pay for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. the pen is mightier than sword

*** 

reach the customer's total payment by calculating the discount on each item. the handcrafted table, priced at $200, is reduced by 15% to $170. the factory-made chairs, priced at $150, are reduced by 15% to $127.50. the customer pays $170 + $127.50 = $297.50 for both items. the pen is mightier than sword

*** 

the customer pays $297.50 for the handcrafted table and the factory-made chairs during the promotional week. the total amount is calculated by applying the 15% discount to each item separately. the pen is mightier than sword
